title: pachypleuria parvula
intro: Pachypleuria parvula, also known as the East Asian polypore, is a species of fungus in the family Thelephoraceae. This plant is primarily known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pachypleuria parvula include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Pachypleuria parvula are often used in traditional medicine, and it can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application in various forms.
